title: kill output data
description: Shape of the ``data`` field on a ``kill`` envelope.

requested_job_ids.description: The job IDs the kill was requested against.
requested_job_ids.title: Requested Job Ids

confirmed_gone_job_ids.description: Subset verified no longer known to the scheduler after the request.
confirmed_gone_job_ids.title: Confirmed Gone Job Ids

still_alive_job_ids.description: Subset still known to the scheduler (e.g. no cancel affordance, or a verification failure — counted honestly as NOT gone).
still_alive_job_ids.title: Still Alive Job Ids

backend_cancel_attempted.description: Whether a scheduler cancel command was actually dispatched through the seam.
backend_cancel_attempted.title: Backend Cancel Attempted

backend_cancel_available.description: Whether the backend seam exposes a cancel affordance at all. False today for every built-in backend — the missing cancel is an integration item.
backend_cancel_available.title: Backend Cancel Available

summary.description: Honest headline, e.g. '3 requested, 0 confirmed gone'.
summary.title: Summary

requested_at.description: When the kill intent was journaled (ISO-8601 UTC).
requested_at.title: Requested At

confirmed_at.description: When the verified-gone subset was journaled (ISO-8601).
confirmed_at.title: Confirmed At

settled.description: True when a FULL kill (everything confirmed gone, nothing still alive) was settled through reconcile — the journal marked terminal and the terminal harvest fired exactly once. False for a partial kill (run still live) or when the best-effort reconcile settle failed (the kill result still stands).
settled.title: Settled
